macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Fed Holds Rates Steady at 3.50-3.75% Amid Rising PPI and Oil Prices
items.summary: The Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged at 3.50-3.75% during the week of March 23, 2026, while PPI rose 0.7% month-over-month. Markets shifted from expecting two rate cuts in 2026 to a 50% chance of a hike as oil prices surged 70%.[1]

items.title: S&P 500 Down Only 3% Despite Sector Dispersion and Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: The S&P 500 declined just 3% for the week amid widening stock dispersion, with energy up 33% YTD due to Middle East conflict and oil surge, while software down 20% and financials down 11% from yield curve flattening.[1]

items.title: US Indices Approach Critical Fibonacci Supports: Dow at 44,802, S&P at 6,498
items.summary: On March 23, 2026, US indices neared key 0.786 Fibonacci support levels at 44,802 for the Dow and 6,498 for the S&P 500, vital to hold to avoid deeper declines.[2]

items.title: Gold Drops 15% to $4,482 Amid Equity Sell-Off and Margin Calls
items.summary: Gold fell sharply 15% from $5,294 on March 2 to $4,482 by March 20, 2026, alongside equities, likely due to hedge funds selling winners like gold to cover losses in tech and small-caps amid wars and high oil prices.[4]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges 5% After Trump Pauses Iran Strikes
items.summary: Bitcoin jumped to $71,200 on March 23 after President Trump announced a five-day pause on strikes against Iranian power plants, reversing earlier losses. The announcement triggered $415 million in liquidations across leveraged positions within four hours.

items.title: Crypto Market Volatility Driven by U.S.-Iran Tensions
items.summary: Over $1 billion in crypto positions were liquidated following Trump's 48-hour ultimatum to Iran on March 21, with Bitcoin dropping from $75,912 to $68,241. The total crypto market cap lost roughly $55 billion as geopolitical tensions overshadowed market fundamentals.

items.title: Ethereum Climbs to $2,141 Amid Risk Asset Rally
items.summary: Ethereum reached $2,141 on March 23 as Trump's Iran pause sent risk assets surging, while XRP surged to $1.44 following the geopolitical de-escalation announcement.

items.title: Bitcoin Exchange Reserves Hit Seven-Year Low
items.summary: Exchange reserves fell to 2.31 million BTC, a seven-year low, while whale addresses holding over 1,000 BTC expanded to 2,140, indicating institutional accumulation during market volatility.

items.title: Fairfax County Pension Fund Reports Record Crypto Returns
items.summary: Fairfax County Employees' Retirement System announced that its digital assets allocation has become the best-performing investment in the fund's history since first investing in blockchain infrastructure in 2019.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Perplexity Launches AI-Powered Medical Records Search
items.summary: Perplexity has rolled out a new AI-powered search tool for medical records, enhancing healthcare data accessibility. This development is part of the latest health tech advancements highlighted in weekly rundowns.

items.title: Doximity Survey Reveals Doctors' AI Usage Trends
items.summary: A new Doximity survey examines how physicians are incorporating AI into their practices, providing insights into adoption rates and applications in healthcare.

items.title: UPATHLN AI Flags Uncertain Lymph Node Reads for Pan-Cancer Detection
items.summary: Chinese researchers introduced UPATHLN, an AI tool that identifies uncertain lymph node pathology reads to improve pan-cancer sensitivity, published on March 21.

items.title: ChatGPT-4.0 Matches Senior Surgeons in Breast Cancer Q&A Study
items.summary: A study from March 20 found GPT-4.0 performs comparably to senior breast surgeons on 30 cancer-related questions, though it should support rather than replace clinicians.

items.title: Verily Raises $300M in AI Healthcare Funding
items.summary: Verily secured a $300 million funding round, signaling strong investment in AI-driven healthcare pipelines and expansions like Google Fitbit record linkage.

items.title: International Study Refines AI for Leukemia Diagnosis Across 6,206 Patients
items.summary: Researchers refined an AI classifier for acute leukemia (AML, APL, ALL) using data from 20 centers in 16 countries, improving generalizability with outlier detection.

items.title: 10 AI-Native Biotechs Highlighted for 2026 Drug Discovery
items.summary: BioBuzz identifies 10 AI-native biotechs, including Micro CRISPR, poised to revolutionize drug development with closed-loop AI systems and clinical pipelines.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Xbox Partner Preview Scheduled for March 26 with Third-Party Reveals
items.summary: Xbox announced a new Partner Preview event on March 26, 2026, featuring updates on S.T.A.L.K.E.R. 2, Stranger Than Heaven, and other third-party games coming to Xbox and Game Pass. The showcase, the first under new CEO Asha Sharma, starts at 10am Pacific.

items.title: Minecraft Dungeons 2 Announced for 2026 Launch
items.summary: Microsoft and Double Eleven suddenly revealed Minecraft Dungeons 2, a sequel to the 2020 dungeon crawler spinoff, set for release in 2026 on PC, PS5, Switch/Switch 2, and Xbox Series X|S.

items.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced Officially Announced
items.summary: Ubisoft confirmed Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced, a long-rumored remaster, alongside updates like 60fps for AC Unity on PS5/Xbox Series and ongoing work on multiplayer Project Invictus.

items.title: Life is Strange: Reunion Releases March 26 Alongside Other Titles
items.summary: Life is Strange: Reunion launches March 26 as the latest narrative adventure, with a Twin Pack edition; other releases include Screamer, Super Mario Bros. Wonder and Virtua Fighter 5 R.E.V.O. on Switch 2, and Mega Man Star Force Legacy Collection on March 27.

items.title: Nintendo Shadowdrops Xenoblade Chronicles X: Definitive Edition Upgrade
items.summary: Nintendo released a $5 Switch 2 upgrade for Xenoblade Chronicles X: Definitive Edition, boosting it to 4K60fps with texture and load time improvements; physical edition follows on April 16.

items.title: Hellraiser: Revival ESRB Rating Reveals Switch 2 Port
items.summary: Saber Interactive's Hellraiser: Revival received an ESRB rating, signaling an imminent launch and disclosing an unannounced Nintendo Switch 2 port.

items.title: Konami Delists Metal Gear Solid 4 and Peace Walker
items.summary: Konami delisted older versions of Metal Gear Solid 4 and Peace Walker ahead of modern releases, while releasing a Volume 2 reveal and final 3.0 update for MGS Master Collection with Switch 2 enhancements.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Elon Musk Launches Terafab Chip Factory in Texas
items.summary: Elon Musk announced Terafab, a $20 billion-plus semiconductor fab in Austin, Texas, jointly developed by Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI to produce custom chips for EVs, robots, and computing. The facility aims to address chip shortages and enable terawatt-scale production near Tesla's headquarters.

items.title: Elliott Management Builds Multibillion Stake in Synopsys
items.summary: Elliott Management has taken a multibillion-dollar stake in Synopsys and is pushing the chipmaking software company to better monetize its offerings amid the tech buildup. Synopsys shares rose 1.6% on the news.

items.title: NASA Announces 'Ignition: NASA’s Plan for The Moon' Event
items.summary: NASA is hosting a public event on March 24, 2026, at its headquarters to outline accelerated preparations for America's return to the Moon's surface by 2028.

items.title: India Advances Semiconductor Technology Initiatives
items.summary: India is making progress in semiconductor technology to turn its manufacturing ambitions into reality, amid efforts to build domestic capabilities.
